title: Head of Compliance, Singapore
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us in Head of Compliance, Singapore to take your Compliance career to the next level with a global market leader.

Make your mark in Global Legal, Compliance and Regulatory (GLCR)

Our Global Legal, Compliance and Regulatory (GLCR) team provides first class advice to colleagues and functions across the organization – ensuring AIG navigates an ever-changing landscape that spans state, federal, and international lines. The team has a multi-dimensional impact, from helping avoid legal exposure to collaborating with other functions to develop cutting-edge new products.

How you will create an impact 

Reporting directly to the Chief Compliance Officer for APAC and China, you will be the senior compliance leader for AIG Singapore. You will have full responsibility for the entire compliance framework, acting as the key interface with our Singapore business stakeholders and relevant regulatory bodies, including the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS). 

  • Lead and own the establishment and/or enhancement of compliance governance initiatives for AIG Singapore. To liaise with process owners to discuss assessment and observations, including efforts related to development and implementation of applicable Compliance policies and procedures. 

  • Partner with business and functional colleagues to help achieve sustainable, profitable growth by providing advice on key compliance risks to the business; devise practical measures to comply, strategies for execution, assist with control design and governance; and align resources to manage risk intelligently. 

  • Devise proactive plans around managing regulatory implications of changes and engaging key stakeholders regarding such changes. 

  • Partner with relevant stakeholders to monitor and implement regulatory changes impacting AIG Singapore and ensure senior management and Board are updated on these developments. 

  • Perform the Annual Compliance Risk Assessment (CRA) in accordance with requirements from the Global Compliance Group and work with Local Stakeholders to review/ address high compliance risks areas identified from the CRA.   

  • Conduct compliance related training in accordance with the Training Plan. Ensure completion of relevant AIG Compliance training programs. 

  • Policies & Procedures: Implement new and updated policies and procedures and ensure that all governance procedures are completed to localize the policies in Singapore. 

  • Sanctions Screening: Undertake and supervise the overall sanctions screening process, working closely with Financial Crime Group for escalation of potential matches. Perform periodic review as required by FCG and General Insurance Sanctions Processing (GISP). 

  • Compliance Records Management: coordinate with business units to resolve issues and gaps, run the Records and Information Management week.

  • Perform the role of a Business Continuity Process Coordinator and undertake all related activities. 

  • Undertake Periodic Reviews exercises and risk assessments. 

  • As required, lead or assist with any special investigations that may be necessary. 

  • Prepare all management reporting material for monthly and quarterly reporting for management committees and the board. 

  • Work with the Data Analytics team to ideate and develop tools to monitor compliance and/or regulatory risks. 

  • Implement and ensure compliance with the overall AIG Compliance framework. 

  • Assist the regional Chief Compliance Officer in Compliance-related queries and projects, when required. 

  • Provide oversight and guidance to the Compliance Manager who reports into this role. 

 

What you’ll need to succeed 

  • 8 - 10+ years of relevant compliance experience in Compliance, Legal or Audit. Preferably in an insurance company. 

  • A professional educational background; preferably legal or audit qualifications. 

  • Ability to work effectively within a matrix organization and with employees of all levels. 

  • Approachable, self-initiator, problem solving with the ability to confront issues diplomatically. 

  • Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the presence and ability to interact and build credibility across all levels of staff. 

  • Strong technical knowledge of MAS regulations and reporting requirements.  

  • Understanding of insurance processes, including the applicability of key laws and regulations. 

  • Ability to work independently while assuming ownership of assigned tasks.

#LI-RO1

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters

At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

Welcome to a culture of inclusion

We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations.

AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.

AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities.  If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.

CP - Compliance

Business responsibilities

  • Assisting in various matters, ensuring compliance with the relevant laws and regulations, including but not limited to:

    • Insurance claims;

    • Insurance products;

    • Distribution channels;

    • Contractual matters (including but not limited to employment issues);

    • Customer servicing; and

    • Various other day-today matters. 

Corporate governance responsibilities:

  • Includes appointment as Company Secretary.

  • Duties include assisting with:

    • preparation and review of board and committee meeting materials and minutes;

    • preparation for the MAS Annual Visit with the Board of Directors;

    • keeping track of appointments and resignations of directors and key executives; and

    • preparation of applications and notifications to the MAS under the Insurance Act and the Insurance (Corporate Governance) Regulations.

Regulatory responsibilities:

  • Keep abreast of regulatory and legislative changes impacting the Company, including but not limited to those in relation to financial institutions as well as corporate governance matters.

  • Assisting to ensure timely notifications to authorities as well as compliance with statutory filings and MAS requirements.

  • Preparation of quarterly regulatory alerts.

What you’ll need to succeed

  • Proven experience in the insurance space, managing in-house corporate governance, regulatory and any other insurance related matters.

  • Self-motivated, pro-active and a team player

  • Able to interact confidently with colleagues at all levels

  • Discreet and diplomatic

  • Committed and reliable

  • Flexible

title: Technical Assistant, Specialty
description:
Technical Assistant, Specialty

At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as an Underwriter, Property to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.

Make your mark in Talbot

With offices in the U.S., London and Asia Pacific, Talbot is the managing agent of Syndicate 1183 at Lloyd’s. Syndicate 1183 underwrites a number of classes of business including war and terrorism, energy, marine, financial lines, cyber, transport, political risk, crisis management, kidnap & ransom, contingency, property and treaty reinsurance. Through its expert team of underwriters and a balanced, geographically-diverse portfolio of business, Talbot has earned a reputation as an industry-leading insurance and reinsurance specialist.

How you will create an impact

  • To ensure all documentation to be scanned and indexed into the DMS system that comes through the Core Team.
  • To ensure data entry is in accordance with agreed guidelines, e.g. endorsements, quotes, promise lines
  • To ensure consistent field use within the Core team.
  • To ensure accuracy and the resolution of any grey areas, of data entry manuals or and escalating any Issues.
  • To ensure accuracy and timeliness of entry for endorsements.
  • Attend and prepare necessary documentation for Core team meetings and for the monthly Operations meeting.
  • To be a SME and/or Super user on Underwriting Systems as required.
  • Ensure both personal and allocated class(es) compliance with document retention policy.
  • To build and maintain close working relationships with Underwriters and Capita.
  • To act in accordance with all regulatory standards and principals
  • Ad-hoc and general administration duties as and when required
  • To run, update and upload the PMD report to Lloyds before the 15th of each month.

Premium Management

  • To prepare, produce and distribute weekly premium management reports such as large movement and signed variance reports.
  • To liaise and work with all Underwriting Teams in all areas of Premium Management data integrity/ allocation and answer any queries they may have.
  • Responsible for USM processing and corrections where applicable
  • To process and validate risks signed through Xchanging; validation of all Underwriting Signing Messages (USMs).
  • To carry out daily posting, to ensure end of month process posting deadlines are reached.
  • Liaise with UTS, Underwriting, Xchanging and Brokers on processing queries, action and monitor until resolved.
  • Identify and liaise with underwriters and chase as appropriate, for partial risks where premium has been processed through Xchanging.

What you’ll need to succeed

  • Excellent general computer literacy. Competent in Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Outlook
  • Detail-oriented
  • Diploma / Degree in any field. Chartered Insurance Institute preferred.
  • Able to operate independently with little direction or guidance. Self-reliance, use of initiative- a ‘self-starter’.
  • Able to prioritize
  • Strong team working approach.
  • Strong analytical skills. Calm, rational and methodical approach
  • Verbal, written and professional presence and ability to communicate at all levels with excellent negotiation skills

Ready to take your career to the next level? We would love to hear from you.

description:
American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets and manage risks.

We’re also committed to making a positive difference for our colleagues and in the communities where we work and live. We encourage colleagues to give back to the causes they care most about, supporting these efforts through our Volunteer Time Off and Matching Grants Programs.

Get to know the business

At AIG, technology is at the heart of everything we do, from underwriting risks to processing claims. The Information Technology team equips our colleagues with the latest tools to complete their work efficiently and with the highest standards of excellence. The team is responsible for shielding the company’s systems from security risks, while designing technology strategies that enable AIG’s businesses to achieve their goals. AIG’s Information Technology functions include enterprise architecture, software and systems engineering, cybersecurity, and technology risk and compliance.

SDN Automation Engineer

The SDN Automation Engineer will be responsible for designing, deploying, and managing software-defined networking (SDN) solutions that enhance the agility, automation, and security of enterprise network infrastructures. We are seeking a highly skilled SDN Automation Engineer with expertise in SDN technologies to increase network agility, scalability and automation in our enterprise, cloud and data center environments. This role involves developing and optimizing SDN architecture, integrating controller-based networking, network function virtualization (NFV), and intent-based networking to improve scalability and operational efficiency.

Responsibilities

·       Design, configure and maintain SDN solutions and associated infrastructures across data centers and cloud environments.

·       Develop and orchestrate network automation workflows using APIs and tools like Ansible, Python, or Terraform to streamline operations and minimize manual effort.

·       Drive the deployment of intent-based networks, self-healing architecture and zero-touch provisioning capabilities.

·       Troubleshoot complex SDN-related network issues, providing root cause analysis and long-term solutions.

·       Collaborate closely with network and tools architects to integrate SDN with existing WAN, LAN, and cloud infrastructures, as well as with the observability layer.

·       Drive the adoption of automation, monitoring, and observability best practices to support scalable and resilient network operations.

·       Stay abreast of emerging SDN, NFV, and automation technologies to continuously evolve and optimize the enterprise network strategy.

·       Manage complex activities simultaneously to short timescales.

·       Develop scripts and processes to test, deploy and validate changes across all network infrastructure, leveraging Ansible and other approved corporate automation and orchestration platforms.

·       Develop state-of-the-art technology, appropriate to deliver stable and scalable technical solutions.

·       Collaborate with stakeholders to develop consensus, drive results, and execute projects across the organization on time and within budget.

·       Maintain standards and designs for network architecture.

·       Articulate design rationale, flexibly adapt solutions, and iterate designs when required.

·       Implement proof-of-concepts for emerging network automation technologies, in accordance with automation architecture roadmaps.

·       Lead deployment, configuration and maintenance of a network source of truth (NSoT) system.

·       Work closely with network architects, senior management and operations teams.

·       Act as an escalation engineer for high severity network related production issues, work with engineers and various vendors to identify tactical and strategic solutions.

Requirements

·       Bachelor’s degree in information technology, or related field, such as Computer Science or Information Technology or equivalent experience.

·       7+ years of proven experience designing, deploying, and managing large-scale network automation projects, with focus on performance optimization and scalability.

·       Proficiency with SDN platforms such as Cisco ACI, VMware NSX or Juniper Contrail.

·       Strong understanding of network fundamentals (L2/L3, BGP, OSPF, VXLAN, EVPN) and controller-based design.

·       Hands-on experience with automation and scripting tools (e.g., Python, Ansible, RESTful APIs).

·       Familiarity with Terraform and Infrastructure as Code (IaC).

·       Experience with network source of truth tools deployment, configuration and operations, e.g. NetBox or Nautobot.

·       Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps methodologies, e.g. Jenkins, GitLab CI, GitHub Actions etc.

·       Familiarity with cloud networking concepts and services (AWS, Azure, GCP).

·       Certifications such as Cisco CCNP/CCIE and Juniper JNCIP/JNCIE or equivalent.

·       Exposure to container networking and micro segmentation in Kubernetes environments.

·       Independent and self-directed work ethic when participating in a collaborative environment.

·       Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders.

·       Dedicated commitment to service availability and quality user experience.

·       Must be results driven with a strong sense of urgency.

title: Broker Director
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Broker Director to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team. This role is responsible for leading our strategic relationships with a select number of our Global and International Brokers.

Make your mark in Client & Broker Engagement

Our Client and Broker Engagement (CBE) team manages AIG’s major client relationships across all product lines, as well as driving new business activity. The team serves major clients and broker partners through an elevated, personalized experience – our client-centric framework drives a consistent experience and best practice, while allowing flexibility to meet the client’s needs.

How you will create an impact

As a Broker Director, you will be accountable for developing and driving increased retention, a healthy pipeline of new business and an enhanced share of wallet - in line with the agreed parameters of the service agreements with each broker partner. This role helps AIG grow profitably across the UK and product lines in line with a strongly governed framework.

Role Responsibilities:

  • Accountable for developing and maintaining strategic relationships for the UK with one of the Global Brokers

  • Responsible for designing, developing and maintaining strategic relationships and create an ease of doing business with AIG. 

  • Conduct Quarterly Business Review (QBR) with the Broker as appropriate to provide governance of the deliverables under the agreements and drive performance.

  • Lead the development and execution of best practice with Product Lines and other stakeholders
     

  • Best Practice:

    • Production Meetings/Salesforce: Lead the overall value of Distribution for the designated broker to via various means including Production Meetings to drive retention and new business utilising Salesforce;

    • Appetite & Value Proposition: Lead the articulation of our underwriting appetite and differentiating value proposition (including Multinational & Claims First) to the brokers;

    • Engagement: Help connect AIG leadership with key leaders at the Broking Houses;

    • Broker Experience: Review broker surveys and agree actions to improve broker experience;

    • Data/Analytics: Utilise internal data (Salesforce, KPI 2.0 and iDive) and broker data to drive retention, new business opportunities and performance;

    • Pipeline & Growth: ensure leadership and input in the value chain between broker and product (and vice versa) to deliver growth.  High visibility in the broking houses to ensure conversion of NB that product would not be able to deliver on their own

  • In conjunction with Client & Broker Leadership, Profit Centres and ELT (where appropriate), develop and implement strategic and operational business plans for designated broker.

  • Work with AIG’ Product Leaders to create co-owned strategic initiatives / projects to deliver increased sales opportunities and support in creation and implementation of new product launches, portfolio and strategic projects.

  • Lead identification and design of new opportunities for profitable growth, schemes, facilities, joint broker and client propositions and new product ideas, using internal data systems & maximising use of the broker systems.

  • Monitor broker performance and delivery against sales, service plans and specific initiatives.  Accelerate progress where opportune and undertake corrective action where required.

  • Ensure all deals and agreements conform to required regulations.  Ensure internal and external governance structures in place to identify and escalate key issues.

What you’ll need to succeed

  • Broad insurance knowledge across P&C and Specialty

  • Solid profile and reputation in the UK commercial insurance market

  • Strong commercial background

  • Experience of working with Senior Underwriter, Product heads and other senior stakeholders

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills and proven interpersonal skills

  • Sales and business development experience

  • CII preferable

title: Risk Officer - Actuarial Independent Review
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Risk Officer – Actuarial Independent Review to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.

 

Make your mark in Enterprise Risk Management

As well as helping customers to manage risks, AIG faces a wide array of our own risks every day. The Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) team partners across the AIG organization to inform strategic decision-making and to facilitate rational risk-taking that drives profitable growth. ERM ensures we have the right tools, processes and systems in place to identify, measure and manage our risks. As an ERM colleague, you can enhance your acumen by helping to shape and support our approach to risk.

 

How you will create an impact

Reporting to the Chief Risk Officer – Global Head of Insurance Risk, the Risk Officer – Actuarial Independent Review will lead the Actuarial Independent Review team in addition to the responsibilities listed below.

 

  • Drive the independent review of AIG Policyholder Liabilities by collaborating with various global actuarial teams and documenting/presenting the results for various boards and committees. 

  • Drive the independent review for selected high priority Pricing and Underwriting Models and communicate the model review findings to Enterprise Risk Management leadership, Actuarial, Underwriting and other key stakeholders.

  • Assist with developing model governance policies and model risk reporting standards.

  • Provide insight and support for technical review of other non-actuarial models when requested.

  • Mentor and manage a high performing team of professionals and bring an inclusive perspective to the workplace.  Promote professional and technical skill development within the team.

  • Support various training and skill development initiatives across AIG.

 

What you will need to succeed

  • University undergraduate degree and a Fellow of Casualty Actuary Society or equivalent.

  • A minimum of 15 years of actuarial experience which evidences deliverables in actuarial reserving and pricing processes focused on the property and casualty industry.

  • Knowledge and experience with international actuarial best practices.

  • Strong analytical skills, knowledge of drivers of risk and knowledge of the insurance market.

  • Ability to build proactive working relationships with stakeholders across various departments and effect change through use of influencing skills.

  • Proven ability to maintain independent perspective and challenge management on risk-related decisions.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to convey complex findings to diverse stakeholders.  The ability to communicate complex matters in a simple and clear manner.

  • A proven history of successful leadership in managing an international team.

 

Ready to make a bigger impact? We look forward to reviewing your application.

For positions based in New York, NY, the base salary range is $180,000-$240,000.  Compensation for this position includes base salary and eligibility for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: US Benefits Overview.

As an ERM colleague, you can enhance your acumen by helping to shape and support our approach to risk.

 

How you will create an impact

Reporting to the Chief Risk Officer – Global Head of Insurance Risk, the Risk Officer – Actuarial Independent Review will lead the Actuarial Independent Review team in addition to the responsibilities listed below.

 

  • Drive the independent review of AIG Policyholder Liabilities by collaborating with various global actuarial teams and documenting/presenting the results for various boards and committees. 

  • Drive the independent review for selected high priority Pricing and Underwriting Models and communicate the model review findings to Enterprise Risk Management leadership, Actuarial, Underwriting and other key stakeholders.

  • Assist with developing model governance policies and model risk reporting standards.

  • Provide insight and support for technical review of other non-actuarial models when requested.

  • Mentor and manage a high performing team of professionals and bring an inclusive perspective to the workplace.  Promote professional and technical skill development within the team.

  • Support various training and skill development initiatives across AIG.

 

What you will need to succeed

  • University undergraduate degree and a Fellow of Casualty Actuary Society or equivalent.

  • A minimum of 15 years of actuarial experience which evidences deliverables in actuarial reserving and pricing processes focused on the property and casualty industry.

  • Knowledge and experience with international actuarial best practices.

  • Strong analytical skills, knowledge of drivers of risk and knowledge of the insurance market.

  • Ability to build proactive working relationships with stakeholders across various departments and effect change through use of influencing skills.

  • Proven ability to maintain independent perspective and challenge management on risk-related decisions.

  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to convey complex findings to diverse stakeholders.  The ability to communicate complex matters in a simple and clear manner.

  • A proven history of successful leadership in managing an international team.

 

Ready to make a bigger impact? We look forward to reviewing your application.

For positions based in New York, NY, the base salary range is $180,000-$240,000.  Compensation for this position includes base salary and eligibility for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. title: Financial Analysis Manager
description:
Finance professionals play a critical role in supporting the demands of multiple constituents, including AIG executives, regulators, and stakeholders, while staying at the forefront of accounting and reporting requirements and compliance strategies. The role oversees expenses covering entire AIG companies and affiliates, including business driven, IT, functional and corporate costs.

This is critical role in Global GOE FP&A interacting directly with Global GOE FP&A Leader, C&B and HR team, Controllership team, Regional Finance teams, and Finance Business Partners.  The ideal candidate should be highly motivated and have experience with quantitative and strategic analysis for C-Suit executives. As a senior finance professional, you are respected for your technical expertise and/or ability to oversee programs and processes. You balance the needs of multiple stakeholders, create strong analytics using data and experience, contribute to strategic initiatives, and offer solutions to identified problems.

Your contribution at AIG

Senior Finance Professionals are responsible for developing financial reporting and analytics to support business strategy execution.  You reach across geographical and functional organizations to seek support for the financial reporting and analytics you produce.

Responsibilities Include:

  • Perform quarterly forecast and actual expense walks using Consistent/Operational Cost Explanations, methodology and definitions that can translate to actionable business partner drivers
  • Partner with business support finance teams to understand and describe the key operational drivers and prepare Q&A for potential questions.
  • Review, analyze, and challenge Forecast/Budget results and identify cost efficiency actions
  • Develop Overall Expense Pulse (including C&B Pulse) estimate and prepare monthly actual comparison to forecasts and budgets
  • Assist with the creation and communication of the Global FP&A GOE calendar to keeping everyone aware of key tasks, dates, and deliverables.
  • Corporate & Segment Reporting – implement a process to facilitate external segment reporting and analysis for expense drivers
  • Develop and maintain historical data models and financials to support the key AIG Financial Reporting variance drivers

What we are looking for

  • 5+ years of experience in expense management; experience in financial services, full insurance P&L experience is a plus, prior consulting experience is a plus;
  • Exceptional/advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills (including Think-cell or similar PowerPoint tools), ability to work with large data analysis and sophisticated leadership presentations requiring quick turnarounds
  • Ability to integrate strategic and operational information into quantitative analysis [and succeed with time, resource and information constraints]
  • Ability to communicate complex ideas to senior finance leaders, and a desire to make a meaningful impact in a dynamic, fast-paced environment
  • Experience in navigating/influencing a global team
  • Strong attention to detail, organization and analytical skills
  • Highly motivated, self-starter and ability to multi-task
  • Team player with good problem-solving skills who is curious and willing to learn, and able to succeed in ambiguous scenarios;
  • Bachelor's degree with a major in a quantitative field (e.g., Finance, Accounting); advanced degree or CFA is a plus.

The base salary range for this position in CT $98,000-122,000 and the base salary range for NJ is $96,000-120,000. The position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: US Benefits

title: Complaints Regulatory Reporting Lead
description:
About the role

The Complaints Regulatory Reporting Lead is a key analytical role that globally sits at the intersection of complaints operations, business insights, regulatory compliance, and data strategy. This role owns the design and delivery of complaint metric reporting across countries and products – translating complaints data into actionable intelligence that drives operational improvement, regulatory adherence, and management decision making. This is an exciting role that collaborates closely with Complaints, Operations, Claims, Compliance, Technology and senior leadership teams to ensure the organization understands not just what is happening – but why and what to do about it.

Key responsibilities:

  • Design and maintain complaint metric frameworks that go beyond volume tracking to surface root causes, emerging themes, and systemic process failures.

  • Produce thematic analysis reports that translate complaint patterns into clear, actionable narratives for management – identifying the underlying drivers of complaint activity and recommending targeted remediation.

  • Partner with underwriting and claims metric teams to develop trend analyses and early warning indicators that enable proactive rather than reactive management of complaint risk.

  • Build and maintain reports that measure regulatory compliance performance across jurisdictions, including adherence to resolution timeframes, acknowledgement and notification obligations, and outcome requirements.

  • Track and report on workforce capacity metrics – resourcing levels, caseloads, and throughput – to support operational planning and identify capacity risks before they affect service quality or compliance.

  • Maintain internal compliance scorecards aligned to the country-level requirements and prepare ad-hoc data extracts and schedules for various regulatory submissions.

  • Conduct ongoing data quality analysis to assess the consistency, completeness, and accuracy of complaint data field usage across in-country and product teams.

  • Define and enforce data standards and field usage conventions, working with complaint teams to remediation inconsistencies and embed good data hygiene at the point of capture.

  • Identify data gaps that limit reporting capabilities and partner with Complaint and Technology teams to resolve.

  • Champion the use and adoption of tools, automation, and dashboard technologies that improve reporting quality, efficiency, accessibility, and timeliness.

  • Partner with in-country and business teams to harmonize reporting methodologies while accommodating local regulatory requirements.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Data Analytics, or related field

  • 7+ years of reporting experience in operations, complaints management, or regulatory compliance within insurance or financial services

  • Strong understanding of complaints regulatory frameworks across one or more jurisdictions

  • Demonstrated ability to design reports and dashboards that drive decisions, not just describe activity

  • Strong data quality and governance instincts, with experience defining field-level standards and remediating inconsistencies as scale.

  • Excellent analytical skills with the ability to navigate between granular data and executive-level narrative

  • Clear, confident communicator with experience presenting complex findings to senior management and compliance stakeholders

  • Proficiency in data visualization and reporting tools – advanced Excel, Cognos, SQL, PowerBI, or similar platforms

For positions based in NJ, the base salary range is $140,000 to $155,000 and the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: :  2026 Benefits Overview

title: HR Business Partner
description:
The Human Resources Business Partner (HRBP) is responsible for taking an outside-in approach on current and future human capital challenges. This requires business credibility, the ability to partner with leaders, and a deep HR and OD functional knowledge. This role will partner with leaders on strategic business priorities and develop and execute short- and long-term organizational effectiveness strategies to achieve targeted business and talent outcomes. The HRBP will provide information and insight to other HR leaders on these priorities and strategies. This role will engage with businesses/functions and be directly responsible for delivering and supporting the human capital programs and processes across a business/function. In doing so, the HRBP will partner with the Senior HRBP's, COEs, HR Operations and HRSS to provide human resources support in the delivery and management of effective HR solutions including performance management, employee training and development, diversity/inclusion initiatives, employee relations and individual coaching. The HRBP will identify and escalate service delivery gaps to enable a One-HR approach. 

 

In addition, the role will advise and coach leaders on driving the organizational values and culture. The role will be able to differentiate between the needs and wants of the business and execute on the priorities for HR.  The HRBP’s expertise on the execution of the human capital initiatives is critical to future company success.

 

Key Responsibilities:

  • Executes and drive the people agenda across your respective client groups.

  • Manages and executes HR programs and HR calendar of events (e.g., compensation review, internal mobility, employee relations, succession planning, employee development) within area(s) of responsibility.

  • Collaborates with HRBPs to deliver a One-HR approach to all activities.

  • Ensures consistency and completion for all HR processes and requirements throughout a specific scope of population. 

  • Ensures accuracy and timeliness in delivery of HR information and tools for business objectives and for enterprise-wide guidelines. 

  • Provides effective input and communication with the team and business/function leaders. 

  • Operates with a focus on increased collaborative trust, employee engagement, leadership effectiveness, and workforce productivity.

  • Executes engagement methods that better facilitate teams to work effectively across the enterprise.

  • Solves problems that lie at the interface of technology, people, and performance.

 

Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of HR Business Partnering and broad cross-functional HR experience.

  • Demonstrated human resources business partnership and organizational design experience, insurance experience is a plus.

  • Experience collaborating with leaders in a large, global company operating in multiple locations, countries, and regions.

  • Strong commercial skills with a successful track record that shows business impact through human capital initiatives.

  • Demonstrated results orientation and the intensity and drive to achieve excellence in strategy and execution.

  • Ability to balance competing priorities and drive strategic initiatives that cross organizational boundaries.

  • Proven ability to lead a workstream that will streamline a process or implement a strategy in a short to mid-term basis (1 – 3 years).

  • Excellent project management, organization, prioritization, and multitasking skills are critical for success.

  • Business acumen and an understanding of the intricacies of the business and the implications of different business models.

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, Human Resources, or related field and/or equivalent combination of experience: insurance or financial services industry preferred.

  • Global experience.

  • Experience with large employee base in multiple sites, countries, or regions.

 

 

 

For positions based in NYC, the base salary range is $105,000 - $135,000. For positions based in NJ, the base salary range is $100,000 to $130,000 and the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: :  2026 Benefits Overview

  • Manages and executes HR programs and HR calendar of events (e.g., compensation review, internal mobility, employee relations, succession planning, employee development) within area(s) of responsibility.

  • Collaborates with HRBPs to deliver a One-HR approach to all activities.

  • Ensures consistency and completion for all HR processes and requirements throughout a specific scope of population. 

  • Ensures accuracy and timeliness in delivery of HR information and tools for business objectives and for enterprise-wide guidelines. 

  • Provides effective input and communication with the team and business/function leaders. 

  • Operates with a focus on increased collaborative trust, employee engagement, leadership effectiveness, and workforce productivity.

  • Executes engagement methods that better facilitate teams to work effectively across the enterprise.

  • Solves problems that lie at the interface of technology, people, and performance.

 

Qualifications:

  • 5+ years of HR Business Partnering and broad cross-functional HR experience.

  • Demonstrated human resources business partnership and organizational design experience, insurance experience is a plus.

  • Experience collaborating with leaders in a large, global company operating in multiple locations, countries, and regions.

  • Strong commercial skills with a successful track record that shows business impact through human capital initiatives.

  • Demonstrated results orientation and the intensity and drive to achieve excellence in strategy and execution.

  • Ability to balance competing priorities and drive strategic initiatives that cross organizational boundaries.

  • Proven ability to lead a workstream that will streamline a process or implement a strategy in a short to mid-term basis (1 – 3 years).

  • Excellent project management, organization, prioritization, and multitasking skills are critical for success.

  • Business acumen and an understanding of the intricacies of the business and the implications of different business models.

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, Human Resources, or related field and/or equivalent combination of experience: insurance or financial services industry preferred.

  • Global experience.

  • Experience with large employee base in multiple sites, countries, or regions.

For positions based in NJ, the base salary range is $100,000 to $130,000 and the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. title: Program Manager
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Program Manager to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.

Make your mark in Programs

With over 50 years in the Programs space, AIG Programs is an industry leader in the U.S. Program business. We focus on developing strategic solutions designed to make Program Administrators’ lives easier. By leveraging our breadth of offerings and dedicated Underwriting, Claims and Loss Control teams, we offer exceptional service and solutions. We serve niche markets on both an admitted and non-admitted basis, through market leading infrastructure and underwriting expertise.

How you will create an impact

A Program Manager is highly motivated, results oriented problem solver who exemplifies a willingness to be flexible, learn on the job and maintain a “can do” attitude.  Program Manager is responsible for the underwriting, marketing & portfolio management of each assigned Program.

  • Developing and negotiating business plans for each assigned program, including plans for new business, renewal rate management, expected retentions to position the division to achieve profit objectives. 
  • Participating in Underwriting Quality Reviews; conduct additional file reviews as needed.
  • Positioning program growth in jurisdictions that are deemed rate adequate and managing to the technically correct price.
  • Coordinating and ‘chair’ Stewardship meetings for assigned Programs.
  • Identifying and marketing new products to round out existing portfolios and incrementally enhance portfolio of products afforded to our clients. 
  • Monitoring renewals, rate targets vs. achieved, retentions, trends, lost business, etc. on a monthly basis to assure achievement of business plan and profitability targets.
  • Understanding each assigned program:  the industries and their exposures to loss, updating program ‘eligibility’ and coverages afforded as needed to minimize adverse exposures to unanticipated loss. 
  • Actively identify training opportunities at the PA level to enhance their LOB knowledge and successfully guide Program Administrators in their execution on risk selection, pricing, coverage terms and conditions in a manner that is aligned with authorities granted by AIG.
  • Actively understanding and managing the authority delegated by the PA to the underwriters assigned to our program(s).
  • Oversight and monitor PA ‘peers review’ process.
  • Developing a strong working knowledge of AIG operating systems (rating and policy issuance) at the PA level.
  • Understanding, managing and minimizing AAL for each program.
  • Developing   line of business expertise for assigned Program(s).
  • Identifying trends in the program profiles, communicating & implementing appropriate measures by LOB in order to protect the portfolio and AIG from adverse exposures.
  • Demonstrating an understanding of industry changes and potential impact by r LOB through clearly articulated recommendations for actions during the account referral process.
  • Ensuring Program Administrator is meeting AIG’s underwriting expectations and is aligned with program business plans through the execution and management of their underwriting authority including the participation in file reviews, UQR Audits and Stewardship meetings.
  • Participating in insurance & industry specific association training/meetings to enhance knowledge by class of business to stay current with industry specific market trends as it pertains to assigned Program(s).
  • Collaborating with Actuarial, Accounting, Claims, Operations, Program Development, Risk Management and Underwriting as necessary.
  • Overseeing the implementation of new business programs assigned to you including coordination with Operations, IT and other functional support areas.
  • Maintaining current industry knowledge, market intelligence and competitor analysis through regular communication with assigned Program Administrator(s) and participation in external conferences, trade shows, etc.
  • Developing & providing superior customer service and maintaining strong working relationships with Program Administrator(s) to sustain portfolio profitability through regular on-site visits, conference calls, etc.

What you’ll need to succeed

  • 10+ Years of Commercial Underwriting Experience focused on Multi Line Package portfolio management.
  • Ability to manage by influence demonstrating strong communication, relationship management, organizational and analytical skills.
  • Demonstrated proficiency on AIG policy rating/issuance platforms (i.e. Cover-All).
  • Working knowledge of ISO, Admitted and Non Admitted product execution.
  • Proven understanding of manuscript, admitted form language and policy coverage analysis.
  • Demonstrated ability to understand and work with analytical tools, reports and technology.
  • Ability to work in dynamic, rapidly changing environment, adapting to changing requirements and collaborating with a wide range of colleagues to quickly deliver solutions.
  • Highly motivated and results-oriented, proven creative problem solver.
  • Very strong verbal and written communication skills.
  • Travel – 30%

title: Senior Underwriting Property
description:
Sr. Underwriter Property

Who we are

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets and manage risks.

We’re also committed to making a positive difference for our colleagues and in the communities where we work and live. We encourage colleagues to give back to the causes they care most about, supporting these efforts through our Volunteer Time Off and Matching Grants Programs.

Get to know the business.

General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for commercial and personal insurance customers. It includes one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. General Insurance offers a broad range of products to customers through a diversified, multichannel distribution network.

The Property Senior Underwriter function is meant to be a partner to the business segments, offering support, expertise and partnership. The existing diversity of the business portfolio demands from the underwriter specialized expertise in the different business areas. The Property Underwriting team works with the business to support a wide array of products and offerings, through a complex and highly demanding distribution force and network. An underwriter has the responsibility of delivering and materializing the product offer and promise.

About the role

What you need to know:

This position will allow us to continue with the growth, this underwriter will manage a portfolio of USD 8M in GPW (2023 GPW data), contribute to the portfolio management activities and to the accounting closing activities. This position is also important to generate new business opportunities to increase a profitable portfolio, to meet the budget goals and the 3-year business plan growth of 16% for 2024 and 5% for 2025 and 2026. The role requires 3 years of experience but the most important good attitude, ambition to win new business, knowledge in excel, analysis skills, soft skills, and fluent English. The role will include the maintenance and retention of the assigned portfolio with an appropriate service level and the following KPI’s for new business.

Underwriter with technical experience in middle and industrial market in the line of business requested, this position will report to the Manger of the LoB.

Your contribution at AIG

As an underwriter will be part of UW teams. He/she should work in team, learn about all the products offered in the respective line of business, focus on the customer to provide excellence service, and contributes to increase a profitable portfolio. He/she should build business relationships.

Based in Mexico City.

 Duties and Responsibilities

1. Functional Responsibilities:

• Quote submissions.

• Build a profitable portfolio.

• Underwriting and operational excellence across all the business by ensuring adherence to underwriting methodology, risk appetite, line setting, guidelines, business rules and pricing adequacy.

• Promote synergies among all Distribution, other underwriting teams and other support staff.

• Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers, producers, reinsurers and other partners.

• Maintenance portfolio requests, providing in a timely response the information that issuance team request to process requirements, taking account of SLA´s accomplishments as part of service

• Interaction with other AIG areas to provide information requests as: Claims to provide information of policies if it need to continue with the claims process without taking part of the claim determination. With distribution teams to attend technical advisories to brokers if it´s necessary. With Operations to instruct for the issuance of the movements.

• Maintain his/her UW information profile properly documented: including UW summary, authorization if it required, UW analysis and any other documentation to guarantee guidelines accomplishment.

• Attend external clients to promote, train agents and brokers to reinforce business line strategy, strength relations with agents and brokers to increase and create new business opportunities always promoting AIG values and behaviors

• Approach and collaboration with the different internal areas of the company, for the resolution of the services that are outside the response times to achieve the appropriate solution and timely closure of the same always under UW authority and responsibility

• Participate in assigned projects i.e., to improve operation as automatization processes, or any other which needs his/her actively participation

2. Accountabilities:

Achieve his/her individual goal with UW discipline stablished in annual budget (monthly monitoring)

Contribution of Team´s goals for new business strategy and retention portfolio stablished in annual goal (monthly monitoring)

Ensure the properly accomplishment of UW guidelines.

Accomplishment of SLA´s established for all business requests.

Attend internal and external clients which requires UW support.

Participate actively in projects which involves UW participation.

 3. Leadership Skills

Excellent communication skills, both written & verbal

Good presentation skills and file knowledge

Time management – ability to manage & prioritize workload and diary management

Ability to contribute effectively within a team environment and with internal/external stakeholders at all levels

Ability to assist with technical training to team claim handlers as required.

Ability to influence claims and non-claims stakeholders to effectively direct claims strategy.

 4. Technical Skills and Expertise

Office Package: Intermediate or advance level of Excel, Word, Outlook.

Bachelor's Degree in actuary, engineering, administration, or similar information

What we’re looking for:

  • Experience:
    • At least 3 years of experience in Middle and Industrial Market Underwriting or knowledge of Property Terms & Conditions.
    • Medium to advanced English
    • Proven experience in the handling problems with agents, brokers, clients
  • Along with technical skills required for the job, soft skills like effective broker engagement, commitment, teamwork, proactive management, effective time management/prioritization, calendar organization, negotiation skills.
  • Effective analytical and attention to detail.

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters

At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

title: Global Technical Engineering Specialist - Property
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Global Technical Engineering Specialist - Property to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.

Make your mark in Risk Engineering

Through our global, world-class engineering capabilities, AIG provides risk engineering and specialist services to help identify and reduce risks at AIG insured clients and properties. We perform comprehensive risk evaluations and recommend solutions that combine cutting-edge thinking and common-sense practicality to avoid or mitigate exposures. Our mission is to make a difference that directly contributes to our customers’ success. We also actively participate in, and contribute to, the development and fine-tuning of industry standards and codes.

How you create an impact:  

  • Contribute to the development of technical risk engineering guidelines, bulletins, processes, and tools.

  • Maintain current industry knowledge of losses and research of new technologies and emerging risks.

  • Assist with complex technical property questions and plan reviews.

  • Conduct research for technical issues where there is limited or no industry guidance and work in conjunction with Head of Technical Services, Chief Underwriting Office, and Risk Engineering Leadership to determine AIG’s stance on the topic.

  • Lead training and development within risk engineering, underwriting, and external clients and organizations.

  • Manage training enrollment and participation records.

  • Manage the training and technical guideline libraries.

  • Work in conjunction with Head of Technical Services and Risk Engineering Managers to support development of systems and process efficiencies.

  • Maintain and provide oversight of the development of risk engineering tools.

  • Participate in external technical and industry groups (NFPA, CFPS, etc.)

What You’ll Need to Succeed  

  • Bachelor’s degree in Science, Technology, Engineering or Mathematics or equivalent industry knowledge 

  • Minimum fifteen (15) years of highly protected risk, specialized industry and/or boiler and machinery field experience 

  • Knowledge of a wide range of commercial property occupancies, including light hazard and heavy industrial occupancies

  • Strong communication skills-both oral and written  

  • Ability/willingness to travel to client and account meetings as well as technical conferences on occasion as requested.

Soft Skills:

  • Highly motivated and works well without close supervision.

  • The individual must be able to make decisions with and without established guidelines and to provide technical support to risk engineers, underwriters, and clients.   

  • Ability to work closely with various Global Head of Technical Services, Account Teams (including Account Engineers) and Underwriters 

  • Ability to multi-task and complete assignments on-time. 

Hard Skills:

  • Professional Engineer, Certified Fire Protection Specialist Designation, or similar (desired but not required) 

  • Ability to perform reviews of buildings and other structures.

  • Ability to read risk reports, construction drawings, diagrams, printed materials.

  • Familiarity with NFPA, FM, and other industry standards

Additional Requirements:

  • Ability to perform physical activities required for the position (i.e., be on feet and walking 8-10 hours a day, safely work at heights) 

  • Ability to understand and appreciate safety training specific to numerous workplace and construction hazards. 

  • Ability to lift and carry materials and equipment up to 50 lbs.

  • Ability to wear safety equipment required for the position if mentoring at survey (hard hat, safety shoes, safety glasses and hearing protection)

  • Ability to perform physical activities required for the position.

  • Ability/willingness to be on-line on Teams (and green when not in meetings) daily when working as part of an immediate-access support team.

title: Benefits Manager, Canada
description:
Benefits Manager, Canada

 

Join us as a Benefits Manager to make a greater impact through your technical expertise and people skills. This could be the ideal chance to step up into your first Senior level role.

 

Make your mark in Benefits

 

How you will create an impact

 

Reporting to the Director of International Benefits, the Canada Benefits Manager will support the design, execution and governance of integrated benefits, pension, savings and well-being programs that align with organizational goals, values and purpose.

 

The role will also support the Director and the Canada Head of HR assessing needs, evaluating cost-effectiveness opportunities, ensuring regulatory compliance, managing or assisting with audits and related activities in order to be responsive to the Company's goals and to ensure that the Company’s benefits are competitive within the Canadian market.

 

The incumbent will also participate in the ongoing monitoring of the benefit programs, including group insurance, short- and long-term disability, paid time off programs as well as pension and savings plans. This role is comprehensive, working within a fast-paced high volume environment.  A successful candidate will need to build strong partnerships with key stakeholders across the business, become a trusted advisor and thought leader to senior business and HR leaders and work closely with our vendors to deliver exceptional employee experience with a focus of driving engagement and retention. This position is required to be on-site 4 days a week at AIG’s office in Toronto.

 Major Responsibilities and Projects

  • Support projects that impact benefits admin, including resources planning, requirements gathering, user acceptance testing, etc. and assist with configuration updates, audits, and end-to-end testing.

  • Administer benefit, pension, and RRSP enrollments and maintain ongoing updates.

  • Act as the primary point of contact for employee inquiries related to pensions and benefits. Respond to and resolve benefits, RRSP, pension, disability, and leave-related inquiries.

  • Engage with key stakeholders including HR, Payroll, vendors, and employees to resolve complex issues and provide strategic recommendations to senior business and HR leaders.

  • Lead process improvement initiatives by identifying gaps, proposing solutions, and implementing best practices for benefits delivery.

  • Leverage Workday HCM and vendor platforms to analyze plan performance, identify inefficiencies, and implement enhancements that ensure accurate integrations and streamlined event processing.

  • Develop communication strategies that improve employee understanding and engagement with benefits, retirement and well-being programs.

  • Review benefits-related materials (summaries, booklets, contracts, communications) to ensure accuracy and alignment with plan design and policies.

  • Ensure compliance with applicable legislation, employment standards, and internal policies.

  • Keep apprised of market trends, legislative developments, and industry best practices to inform recommendations for program changes

 

Disability, Accommodation, and Leave Management

  • Manage disability claims, ensuring confidentiality, compliance, and compassionate support. Partner with Employee Relations on disability cases where required. Work closely with third-party vendors in managing claims.

  • Manage medical accommodation requests; engaging leaders and employees to ensure support and success of the accommodation plan. Work closely with third-party vendors in managing requests, as required.

  • Provide guidance and advisory support to HR leaders on disability and accommodation practices.

  • Review data and trends to inform continuous program improvement.

 

The ideal candidate must have the following skills:

  • Strong project management skills

  • Exceptional organizational skills with strong attention to detail, follow-up and problem resolution

  • Demonstrated success in a dynamic, frequently changing and culturally diverse environment

  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (verbal, written, presentation) with ability to interact effectively and tactfully with all levels throughout the organization, as well as with outside consultants and vendors

  • Team player with the ability to prioritize, multi-task and maintain flexibility in a fast-paced, service oriented environment

  • Excellent planning, organizational and time management skills


Education and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Human Resources or related field

  • Minimum 10 years of Canadian benefits experience

  • Sound knowledge of benefits regulations and market trends

  • Proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Word and SharePoint

 

Ready to step up to new challenges? We would love to hear from you.

 

The base salary range for this position is $88,000-$132,000 and the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: CA Benefits Overview.

 

AIG Canada is proud to be named one of Greater Toronto's Top Employers for the seventh year in a row. This award demonstrates AIG’s commitment to outstanding human resources initiatives and fostering a forward-thinking workplace.

title: Supervisory Accountant, General Insurance International Controllership
description:
The Supervisory Accountant is integral in supporting AIG’s General Insurance International Controllership team, with primary responsibility for controllership activities related to the preparation and analysis of quarterly consolidated financial statements for the General Insurance International operating segment in accordance with US GAAP and internal policies.  This position is responsible for supporting delivery of consolidated financial information included within the AIG 10-Q/10-K, maintaining data integrity and a robust control environment to provide effective support to the General Insurance International Controllership.  This role will also involve partnering with other stakeholders to build strong relationships and collaborate across various functions of the Company, to support an efficient close process.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Preparing quarterly consolidated financial statements for AIG General Insurance International’s results based on regional submissions, in accordance with US GAAP, for ultimate inclusion within the AIG 10-Q/10-K financial results and segment disclosures
  • Contributing to analytical reviews of segment Balance Sheet and Income Statement  
  • Partnering with Reinsurance, Actuarial, Investment and regional controllers and International segment to ensure transactions are properly reflected in the General Insurance International Consolidated financial statements
  • Performing ledger data analysis and remediation, as well as executing journal entries in conjunction with the preparation of financial results for several legal entities
  • Support the audit process with external and internal auditors.
  • Identifying opportunities and contributing to strengthening the control environment and implementing process improvements
  • Provide surge capacity to the General Insurance Controllership during peak quarterly close periods

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting required
  • Qualified CPA preferred, with a minimum of 3-5 years of relevant professional experience, particularly in a controllership or financial reporting role at a public company; insurance sector experience a plus
  • Strong project management and change management capabilities, with a clear desire to be part of developing solutions and driving matters to completion.
  • Strong attention to detail, where accuracy and precision are critical to success, yet possessing an ability to step back and understand the broader objectives around analysis of results
  • Systems-technical acumen, with a desire and aptitude to learn and master new financial accounting and reporting systems
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills, including presentation development and delivery, with an ability to effectively work across teams
  • Comfortable operating in a dynamic, transformational environment, where change is embraced and viewed as an opportunity for one’s own professional development and to drive process improvement for the team and broader organization

For positions based in New York and Connecticut, the base salary range is $87,000-109,000. For positions based in New Jersey base salary range is $85,000-107,000. In addition, the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan.   • Preparing quarterly consolidated financial statements for AIG General Insurance International’s results based on regional submissions, in accordance with US GAAP, for ultimate inclusion within the AIG 10-Q/10-K financial results and segment disclosures
  • Contributing to analytical reviews of segment Balance Sheet and Income Statement  
  • Partnering with Reinsurance, Actuarial, Investment and regional controllers and International segment to ensure transactions are properly reflected in the General Insurance International Consolidated financial statements
  • Performing ledger data analysis and remediation, as well as executing journal entries in conjunction with the preparation of financial results for several legal entities
  • Support the audit process with external and internal auditors.
  • Identifying opportunities and contributing to strengthening the control environment and implementing process improvements
  • Provide surge capacity to the General Insurance Controllership during peak quarterly close periods

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in accounting required
  • Qualified CPA preferred, with a minimum of 3-5 years of relevant professional experience, particularly in a controllership or financial reporting role at a public company; insurance sector experience a plus
  • Strong project management and change management capabilities, with a clear desire to be part of developing solutions and driving matters to completion.
  • Strong attention to detail, where accuracy and precision are critical to success, yet possessing an ability to step back and understand the broader objectives around analysis of results
  • Systems-technical acumen, with a desire and aptitude to learn and master new financial accounting and reporting systems
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills, including presentation development and delivery, with an ability to effectively work across teams
  • Comfortable operating in a dynamic, transformational environment, where change is embraced and viewed as an opportunity for one’s own professional development and to drive process improvement for the team and broader organization

For positions based in New York and Connecticut, the base salary range is $87,000-109,000. For positions based in New Jersey base salary range is $85,000-107,000. In addition, the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. title: Underwriter Casualty (w/m/d)
description:
Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Österreich ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit einem lokalen Team versierter Experten in Wien jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams in Wien suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) für die Position des Underwriter Casualty (w/m/d) mit einem Pensum von 80 % bis 100 % zur Verstärkung unseres Teams am Standort Wien.

Was Sie erwartet:

  • Underwriting: Prüfung und Bewertung von Haftpflichtrisiken für Industrieunternehmen und globale österreichische Akteure. Laufende Betreuung des bestehenden Portfolios und Angebotslegung für potenzielle Neukunden. Analyse und Dokumentation hinsichtlich Deckungsqualität, Profitabilität, interner Guidelines und Regeln sowie individuellen Risikoprofilen der Unternehmen. Verhandlung der Bedingungen und Konditionen mit Maklern und Kunden.
  • Vertrieb: Aufbau und Pflege einer vertrauensvollen und engen Partnerschaft mit den relevanten Maklerhäusern in Österreich. Strategische Akquisition gemeinsamen mit dem Client und Broker Management für Österreich. Repräsentation der AIG an Events und Fachveranstaltungen.
  • Inhaltliche Arbeit: Laufende Weiterentwicklung der AIG-Bedingungen sowie Beobachtung von Gesetzgebung und Rechtsprechung hinsichtlich Haftungs- und Versicherungsrechtlicher Themen. Aufbau eines tieferen Verständnisses für Industrien und den typischen Risiken und Haftungsszenarien.
  • Arbeit im Team: Enge kollegiale Zusammenarbeit in allen zuvor genannten Bereichen. Engmaschige Einarbeitung sowohl in die Kerndisziplin Underwriting als auch in die Systeme und Governance der AIG.
  • Blick über den Tellerrand: Neben den klassischen Betriebshaftpflichtrisiken besteht auch die Möglichkeit im Bereich Energy, Construction und CPI (Produktschutzversicherung) Erfahrungen zu sammeln und in weiterer Folge Verantwortung zu übernehmen.

Was Sie mitbringen:

  • Abgeschlossene akademische Ausbildung vorzugsweise mit juristischem Schwerpunkt oder mit Versicherungsfachwissen.
  • Berufserfahrung im Bereich Industrieversicherung oder Haftpflicht bei einem Versicherer oder Versicherungsmakler mit Fokus auf Industrierisiken oder Berufserfahrung in einer Rechtsanwaltskanzlei mit Tätigkeitsfeld Zivil- und/oder Versicherungsrecht.
  • Selbstständiges Arbeiten, hohe Selbstmotivation und gute Auffassungsgabe
  • Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift.

Was wir Ihnen bieten und warum wir die beste Wahl sind:

Wenn Sie Ihr Wissen und Ihre Erfahrung in den Bereichen Betriebs- und Produkthaftpflicht sowie Umwelthaftpflicht und Produktschutz vertiefen wollen, sind Sie bei uns richtig. Wir bieten Ihnen vielfältige Aufgaben zur selbst bestimmten Tätigkeit in einem dynamischen und internationalen Umfeld.

Wir bieten ein marktkonformes Gehalt. Für die ausgeschriebene Stelle kommt ein kollektivvertragliches jährliches Bruttomindestentgelt von EUR 56 454,58 bei einer wöchentlichen Arbeitszeit von 38,5 Stunden (Vollzeittätigkeit) zur Anwendung, wobei jedoch in Abhängigkeit Ihrer konkreten Qualifikation Bereitschaft zur Überzahlung besteht. Wenn Sie Teil unseres Teams sein möchten, freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung sowie Angabe Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung und Ihres frühestmöglichen Eintrittstermins über unser Bewerbungsportal.

Bei Fragen erreichen Sie Hanna Gamper unter bewerbungen@aig.com oder +49 (0) 69 97 113 234.

title: Specialist, Underwriting Support
description:
Join us as a Underwriting Support to take your career to the next level with a global market leader.

How you will make an impact

As a Specialist in our Underwriting Operations Team, you will be responsible for providing efficient and high-quality underwriting support to the UK Underwriting Teams. You will support the end-to-end underwriting process through regular liaison with other functions to ensure the highest quality of service is delivered to our brokers and clients.

This is a fantastic role for someone with some insurance experience. You will be given comprehensive training and the opportunity to complete insurance qualifications.

Some of the key responsibilities include:

  • Manage a portfolio of Accounts

  • Efficiently and accurately ensure all new business and renewal accounts are processed

  • Proactive engagement with Underwriting to ensure new and renewed accounts have an efficient and smooth account hand over, ensuring information received is understood and complete in order to process / instruct policies on systems and to the network.

  • Liaise with Underwriters to ensure correct interpretation of data for accuracy and completeness.

  • Maintain documentation and files, ensuring all documentation is retained centrally.

  • Accountable for (RI) premium, (RI) commission and tax bookings in processing systems

  • Liaise with the Credit Control team - answering queries, tracking, and monitoring premium using core underwriting systems.

  • Manage requests originating from clients and brokers, and refer to Underwriting as required.

  • Regular interaction with our off-shore team – leading huddles, auditing work, quality control

  • Build strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders

  • Develop expert level knowledge with regards to systems, and processes and keep abreast of new guidelines published.

  • Act as reference point for others in the team when questions come up and/or uncertainties exist.

  • Support project work to ensure that existing processes meet requirements.

What you’ll need to succeed

  • Minimum A-level standard of education or equivalent business experience

  • Some insurance experience

  • Strong organisation skills with the ability to prioritise work and meet deadlines

  • Collaborative with the ability to build strong relationships with key internal and external stakeholders.

  • Excellent communication skills

  • Strong problem solving skills

  • Attention to detail

  • Strong IT skills - experience of Microsoft Outlook, Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Teams and Power BI would be advantageous

jobPostingInfo.jobDescription:
Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Österreich ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit einem lokalen Team versierter Experten in Wien jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein.

Für die Position des Senior Underwriter Accident & Health (w/m/d) suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) zur Verstärkung unseres Teams an unserem Standort in Wien.

Was Sie erwartet:

  • Underwriting:
    • Risikobeurteilung und Zeichnung von Accident & Health Risiken (z.B. Kollektivunfall, Dienstreiseversicherungen)
    • Produktentwicklung inklusive Aufbau von Tarifen in Abstimmung mit unseren Aktuaren sowie Erstellen von Wordings
    • Entwicklung und Einführung von innovativen Deckungs- und Servicekonzepten als Differenzierungsmerkmal zur Konkurrenz
  • Betreuung von nationalen- und internationalen Programmen inkl. englischsprachiger Kommunikation mit ausländischen Maklern, Kunden und Kollegen des unternehmenseigenen Auslandsnetzwerkes
  • Ausbau und ergebnisorientiertes Managen des bestehenden A&H Portfolios im Rahmen der AIG Underwriting Strategie
  • Selbständiges Führen von Vertragsverhandlungen mit Brokern und Kunden sowie Pflege der Geschäftsbeziehung

Was Sie mitbringen:

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium oder Ausbildung mit versicherungstechnischem Hintergrund
  • Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung in der Risikoprüfung von A&H Risiken sowie im technischen Underwriting, Kenntnisse über internationale Versicherungsprogramme von Vorteil
  • Gute Kenntnis des deutschsprachigen A&H-Marktes sowie überzeugende Marktpräsenz
  • Stilsichere Sprachkenntnisse in Deutsch sowie gute Englisch-Kenntnisse und Gewandtheit im Dialog und Umgang mit Kunden und Maklern
  • Offen für Innovation, pragmatische ‚can do' Haltung und Fähigkeit, aus der Box heraus zu denken
  • Bereitschaft zu regelmäßigen Reisen
  • gute Kenntnisse in MS-Office Anwendungen, insbesondere sehr gute Excelkenntnisse

Was wir Ihnen bieten und warum wir die beste Wahl sind:

Wenn Sie Ihr Wissen und Ihre Erfahrung im Bereich Personenversicherungen (insbesondere Kollektivunfall-, Dienstreise- und Expatversicherung) anwenden und vertiefen wollen, sind Sie bei uns richtig.
