title: Manager, Business Data Analysis
description:
At AIG, we are reimagining the way we help customers to manage risk. Join us as a Manager, Business Data Analysis to play your part in that transformation. It’s an opportunity to grow your skills and experience as a valued member of the team.

Make Your Mark: 

General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services – focused on helping to manage risk for commercial and personal customers. We offer a broad range of products through a diversified, multi-channel distribution network, including one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks.  The Data Office supports the overall General Insurance product by managing Data, Data Governance, and the presentation of the data (Business Intelligence Delivery).

How you will create an impact:

AIG is looking for a driven individual to assume a critical role within the Data Office.  This role will be responsible for working directly with leaders within Commercial Lines functional areas ensuring an understanding of data and analytics needs and priorities.  In addition, this role will have Global Data Warehouse Product Ownership responsibilities ensuring execution is aligned to the highest business priorities and Data Office strategy and that sunset opportunities are being realized.

What we’re looking for:

  • Proven experience partnering with business stakeholders to drive data and analytics transformation initiatives

  • Strategic thinker with problem-solving skills, and innovative rigor

  • Thought leader – ability to define the data and analytic needs of key partners based on business outcomes and align work to strategic data and analytic plans

  • Strong business acumen and understanding of P&C insurance metrics and financials

  • Strong organizational skills including proven ability to drive medium to large projects/programs of work

  • Ability to consult and interact effectively with all levels of the organization and develop strong business partnerships

  • Working knowledge of data management, business intelligence, and data warehousing.

  • Ability to understand the long-term ("big picture") and short-term perspectives of situations.

  • Strong execution and analytical skills

  • Ability to lead a team effectively without organizational alignment

  • Strong program and project management skills

  • Strong knowledge of Agile methodology

  • Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced environment and with changing priorities

  • 6 - 12 years of business intelligence and/or analytical experience in commercial insurance preferred.

  • Degree from a four-year college or university, MS is preferred.

  • Strong problem-solving ability - candidate needs to exhibit structured thinking, attention to details, and analytical skills and process orientations.

  • Understanding of programming and statistical tools.

  • Advanced understanding of P&C Insurance industry 

  • Highly motivated, positive, and collaborative attitude, team player with strong communication skills.

For positions based in New Jersey, the base salary range is $99,000 - $143,000. For positions based in New York, the base salary range is $105,000 - $148,000. For positions based in Illinois, the base salary range is $120,000 - $150,000. For positions based in Massachusetts, the base salary range is $122,000 - $150,000. In addition, the position is eligible for a bonus in accordance with the terms of the applicable incentive plan. In addition, we’re proud to offer a range of competitive benefits, a summary of which can be viewed here: 2026 Benefits Overview

Ready to find new solutions? We would love to hear from you.

title: Finance Operations Process Transformation Leader
description:
Who we are

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets, manage risks and provide for retirement security.

Get to know the business

Our finance professionals play an essential role in maintaining a healthy balance sheet at AIG. They deliver significant finance support for the overall enterprise, optimizing operations, funding and capital structure to honor our commitments to our stakeholders. They inform the decisions of AIG executives and comply with regulatory requirements, maintaining financial discipline, transparency and controls, while providing timely and meaningful information. Within the Finance team, the Treasury and Capital Markets group handles Treasury Department activities and funding the overall capital structure of AIG and investor relations. The Actuarial and Value Management team manages rating agency relations. The Finance Operations team handles controllership activities, including Security & Exchange Commission reporting and tax.

About the role

Reporting to the Finance Lead, this position will work with the global teams involved in building, operationalizing and governing intercompany expense processes with (AIG Operations) regional leaders, third party leaders and global process leaders.

What you need to know:

Finance Transformation Project Leader for Finance Operations

Strategic Thought Leader:

  • Strategic Planning: Align project initiatives with company goals, providing insights to senior stakeholders (e.g., CFO, Program Directors).

  • Drive process insights and improvements, leveraging established frameworks and tools

  • Commercial mindset, focusing on costs, benefits, returns

  • Ability to prioritise, risk assess, focus on key impact and outcomes

  • Flexible and agile in response to dynamic business environments

  • Improve stakeholder-operating teams’ understanding of financial impacts by providing thoughtful and transparent analysis

  • Vendor/Supplier Management: Oversee SOWs (Statements of Work), procurement, and align third-party deliverables with finance teams

  • Senior management approval for implementation, contract signing, managing relationship with external vendor, liaising with multi-functional stakeholders for support and input, providing guidance on project approach, and addressing any potential roadblocks

  • Comfortable transiting out of Excel and PowerPoint, into an integrated planning model execution and governance

Execution Focused Delivery Leader:

  • Financial Control & Reporting: Lead project financial tracking, including budgeting, forecasting, cost control, and variance analysis (Actuals vs. Budget).

  • Governance & Standardization: Establish and enforce PMO processes, frameworks, templates, and best practices across the portfolio.

  • Project Governance: Oversee project delivery milestones, RAID logs (Risks, Actions, Issues, Decisions), and change management.

  • Resource & Capability Planning: Manage capacity planning, team allocation, and cost tracking for project teams.

  • Develop analytics and presentation materials to provide senior management transparency into the functional and process financial trends and impacts

  • Establish and drive financial governance while function or process integration

  • Lead initiatives from concept through execution as a thought leader and a project manager to ensure successful and timely delivery.

What we are looking for:

  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, accounting or a related discipline

  • Significant experience in progressive accounting and / or finance

  • Experience in the areas of strategic planning, budgeting, reporting and profitability analysis.

  • Extensive experience in driving initiatives through to completion

  • Effective written & oral communication skills, influencing skills, and the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams and management to deliver objectives

  • Strong knowledge of financial analytics and KPI metrics

  • Strong quantitative and modeling skills

[German Version below]

This role as Financial Lines Senior Underwriter (f/m/x) will offer a unique opportunity to drive our growth across several Eastern European countries and build our Financial Lines portfolio. We are looking for a talented individual with several years of experience in Financial Lines underwriting. The position requires both an ambition to build relationships across a large region but also the ability to handle multiple submissions from different countries and the desire to build a portfolio from the ground up. The successful candidate will design a strategy, set-up a process and underwrite risks to grow Financial Lines in Eastern Europe.

The position is located in Germany and reports to the Practice Leader Financial Lines Central and Eastern Europe. The goal is to manage and develop a portfolio and lead the organization towards realizing our mission as a leading insurer in Europe with focus on profitable growth.

Main Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Grow Financial Lines’ portfolio of Eastern European risks
  • Build relationship with key stakeholders both internally and externally
  • Operate as the main Financial Lines contact for Eastern European brokers
  • Work closely with the actuarial team to make sure the portfolio is long term sustainable
  • Work closely with the European underwriting managers and the Head of FL Europe in setting a long-term strategy

Requirements:

  • extensive experience in Financial Lines underwriting preferably within multiple products
  • Minimum bachelor’s degree preferably in Business Administration, Finance or Law
  • Have a strong interest in negotiating and in analyzing risks
  • Have a positive, determined approach to researching and analyzing new business opportunities.
  • Be flexible, ambitious and independent
  • Strong written and verbal skills in English. Eastern European language skills is a plus
  • Open to travel and building strong relationships both internally and externally


Why we are the best choice:

We offer you a variety of tasks in a digital and international environment with flexible working hours and smart working. We work in flat hierarchies with real creative freedom. Teamwork is our top priority. Regular Culture & Inclusion events as well as virtual sports activities and bicycle leasing round off our working environment. Additional benefits such as 30 days' vacation per year, days off on December 24 and 31, a company pension scheme and capital-forming benefits are a matter of course for us.

Have we aroused your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your detailed application.

If you have any questions, please reach out to Hanna Gamper (Talent Acquisition Partner DACH) under bewerbungen@aig.com or +49 (0) 69 97113 234.

______________________________________________________________________________

Seit über 100 Jahren stehen wir weltweit für unsere Expertise zur Absicherung komplexer Risiken. In diesem partnerschaftlichen Sinne unterstützen wir unsere Kunden dabei, einer ungewissen Zukunft vertrauensvoll entgegenzublicken. In Deutschland ist die AIG seit 70 Jahren aktiv und steht den Versicherten seitdem mit lokalen Teams versierter Experten an vier Standorten jederzeit zur Seite. Bei uns werden Sie nicht nur Teil eines ambitionierten Teams bei einem der weltweit führenden Versicherungsunternehmen, sondern haben gleichzeitig die Möglichkeit, an der Gestaltung nationaler und internationaler Risikotransfers hautnah beteiligt zu sein.

Für die Position des Senior Underwriter Financial Lines CEE (w/m/d) suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt einen neuen Kollegen (m/w/d) zur Verstärkung unseres Teams an einem unserer deutschen AIG-Standorte.

Was Sie erwartet:

  • Ausbau unserer Geschäftsaktivitäten in einigen Ländern in Osteuropa
  • Aufbau von Kundenbeziehungen mit Maklern und Kunden sowie Anpassung der Financial Lines Produkte an die lokalen Gegebenheiten (Osteuropa)
  • Underwriting (Risikoprüfung und -selektion) von osteuropäischen Risiken in allen Financial Lines Produktbereichen (ausgenommen M&A)
  • Präsentation der AIG gegenüber Kunden und Maklern als flexibler „Solution Provider“

Was Sie mitbringen:

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium der Rechts- oder Wirtschaftswissenschaften (internationale Abschlüsse von Vorteil) oder vergleichbarer Studiengänge
  • Langjährige Berufserfahrung im Bereich Financial Lines (Underwriting und Produktentwicklung) in (Ost-) Europa
  • Vorhandenes Netzwerk von Beziehungen (Kunden und Makler) in Osteuropa
  • Kreativität und Innovationsfreude, um passende Versicherungslösungen in einem sehr dynamischen Risikoumfeld zu erarbeiten
  • Affinität für Versicherungsbedingungen und der damit im Zusammenhang stehenden Marketingaktivitäten
  • Teamfähigkeit und schnelle Auffassungsgabe sowie Entscheidungsfreude
  • Hohe Belastbarkeit besonders beim Arbeiten unter Stress und Termindruck sowie hohes Durchsetzungsvermögen
  • Fließende Englischkenntnisse, weitere osteuropäische Sprachkenntnisse von Vorteil

Warum wir die beste Wahl sind:

Wir bieten Ihnen vielfältige Aufgaben in einem digitalen und internationalen Umfeld mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten. Wir arbeiten in flachen Hierarchien mit echten Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten. Teamarbeit steht bei uns an oberster Stelle. Regelmäßige Culture & Inclusion-Veranstaltungen sowie Corporate Benefits und Fahrradleasing runden unsere Arbeitswelt ab. Zusatzleistungen wie 30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr, arbeitsfreie Tage am 24.12. und 31.12., betriebliche Altersvorsorge und vermögenswirksame Leistungen sind für uns selbstverständlich.

Wir haben Ihr Interesse geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihren Lebenslauf direkt über unser Bewerbungsportal.

Bei Fragen erreichen Sie Hanna Gamper (Talent Acquisition) unter bewerbungen@aig.com oder +49 (0) 69 97113 234.

#LI-AIG

At AIG, we value in-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.

Enjoy benefits that take care of what matters

At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family.

Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world

American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become.

Welcome to a culture of inclusion

We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations.

AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.

AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities.  If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com.  

Functional Area:

UW - Underwriting

AIG Europe S.A. (Germany branch)
